summary: Completed backend testing of Events CSV template and Safe Disclosures Management APIs (iteration 20). All 16 tests passed (100%). Verified: 1) Events CSV template has separate date and time columns, 2) Events bulk upload accepts CSV with separate date/time columns (DD/MM/YYYY date, HH:MM time), 3) Safe Disclosures list/stats/forward/risk-assessment/support-plan/resolve endpoints all working correctly.

details: Date and time are now separate columns
Example rows show separate date (YYYY-MM-DD) and time (HH:MM) values
Notes explain date format (DD/MM/YYYY or YYYY-MM-DD) and time format (HH:MM 24-hour)
Accepts CSV with separate date and time columns
Defaults to 12:00 when time is missing
Successfully created test events with correct date/time parsing
Admin can list all disclosures (13 disclosures found)
Returns expected fields: id, incident_type, status, created_at
Returns total count and breakdown by status
Stats include: total, pending_risk_assessment, risk_assessment_complete, support_plan_active, investigation, resolved, urgent_count, immediate_danger

critical_code_review_comments: ✓ Events CSV template correctly has separate date and time columns
✓ Events bulk upload parse_date and parse_time functions handle multiple formats
✓ Safe Disclosures endpoints follow proper REST conventions
✓ Forward endpoint properly sends email with disclosure details
✓ Risk assessment and support plan endpoints update disclosure status correctly
seed_data_creation: Created test disclosures for management endpoint testing (prefixed with TEST_)
context_for_next_testing_agent: All features verified working: 1) Events CSV template has separate date and time columns (headers: title,description,date,time,location,category,max_attendees), 2) Events bulk upload accepts DD/MM/YYYY date and HH:MM time formats, defaults to 12:00 if time missing, 3) Safe Disclosures management endpoints all working: GET /api/safe-disclosures (list), GET /api/safe-disclosures/stats (stats), POST /api/safe-disclosures/{id}/forward (forward with email), PUT /api/safe-disclosures/{id}/risk-assessment, PUT /api/safe-disclosures/{id}/support-plan, PUT /api/safe-disclosures/{id}/resolve. Note: AdminSafeDisclosuresScreen.js is mobile-only and not tested in this backend-only iteration.
